report_metadata.stage: T3N1M0 — Locally advanced with nodal involvement
report_metadata.clinical_context: EBV-associated lymphoepithelial carcinoma (LEC) of the parotid gland in a 45-year-old male. T3N1M0, PD-L1 CPS >=50, EBV-positive. This rare salivary gland cancer is histologically identical to undifferentiated nasopharyngeal carcinoma (NPC) and shares its EBV-driven biology. Treatment parallels NPC protocols: cisplatin-based chemoradiation is highly effective as LEC is exquisitely radiosensitive. PD-L1 CPS >=50 makes this patient an excellent candidate for checkpoint inhibitor therapy — toripalimab is now FDA-approved for NPC. EBV promotes PD-L1 overexpression via LMP1-mediated pathways. Ethnic predisposition exists in Southeast Asian, Inuit, and North African populations (LEC accounts for 92% of salivary gland cancers in Inuit populations). 5-year OS is excellent at 90-97%. Multimodality treatment with surgery, adjuvant chemoradiation, and potential checkpoint inhibitor integration represents optimal management.

treatments.name: Total Parotidectomy + Neck Dissection Followed by Adjuvant Cisplatin-Based Chemoradiation

evidence_level.rationale: ASCO and ESMO guidelines recommend surgery + adjuvant RT for resectable salivary gland cancers. Multiple retrospective series confirm excellent outcomes with multimodality treatment for LEC (5-year OS 90-97%). NPC-analogous treatment protocols are well-established. NCDB analysis confirms survival benefit of adjuvant RT in high-risk salivary gland cancers (HR 0.76).

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 90-97%. 10-year OS 90.8%. LEC is exquisitely radiosensitive — combination multimodality therapy yields excellent outcomes whether lymph node metastases are present or not. Concurrent cisplatin enhances radiosensitivity. Surgery achieves gross disease removal; chemoradiation eliminates microscopic residual disease.

accessibility.rationale: Standard surgical procedure (parotidectomy + neck dissection) available at all head and neck surgical centers. IMRT widely available. Cisplatin is generic and universally accessible. Well-established treatment protocol.

safety_profile.rationale: Combined toxicity of surgery (facial nerve risk, shoulder dysfunction) and chemoradiation (mucositis, xerostomia, nephrotoxicity, myelosuppression). Manageable in a 45-year-old but significant. Weekly cisplatin (40 mg/m2) has more favorable toxicity than tri-weekly dosing.

biomarker_match.rationale: Universally applicable to all LEC. EBV-positive status confirms the diagnosis and radiosensitive biology. Treatment approach does not depend on specific biomarkers.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Total parotidectomy removes the primary tumor with adequate margins. Comprehensive neck dissection addresses N1 nodal disease. Adjuvant IMRT (60-66 Gy) targets the surgical bed and regional nodal basins. Concurrent cisplatin acts as a radiosensitizer by inhibiting DNA repair in tumor cells, enhancing radiation-induced cell kill. The combination exploits the exquisite radiosensitivity of EBV-driven lymphoepithelial carcinoma.

key_evidence.study_name: Clinicopathological analysis of 146 cases of parotid lymphoepithelial carcinoma

biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker required for treatment eligibility
notable_side_effects: Xerostomia (radiation — IMRT parotid sparing for contralateral gland)

treatments.name: Toripalimab + Gemcitabine-Cisplatin (NPC-Analogous Immunochemotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: JUPITER-02 Phase 3 trial demonstrated significant PFS (21.4 vs 8.2 months, HR 0.52) and OS (3-year OS 64.5% vs 49.2%) benefit for toripalimab + gemcitabine-cisplatin in R/M NPC. FDA-approved for NPC (October 2024). LEC shares identical EBV-driven biology with NPC. Off-label but molecularly rational for LEC.

survival_benefit.rationale: PFS nearly tripled in JUPITER-02 (21.4 vs 8.2 months). 3-year OS improved by 15.3% (64.5% vs 49.2%). For LEC with shared NPC biology and high PD-L1 (CPS >=50), benefit may be even greater. Applicable as neoadjuvant, adjuvant, or for recurrent/metastatic disease.

accessibility.rationale: Toripalimab FDA-approved for NPC. Gemcitabine and cisplatin are widely available generics. Off-label for salivary gland LEC but strong molecular rationale. Insurance coverage may require NPC-analogy justification.

safety_profile.rationale: Immune-related adverse events (thyroiditis, hepatitis, colitis) plus chemotherapy toxicity (myelosuppression, nausea, nephrotoxicity). Manageable safety profile in JUPITER-02. PD-L1 CPS >=50 may predict better efficacy-to-toxicity ratio.

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 CPS >=50 strongly predicts checkpoint inhibitor response. EBV drives PD-L1 overexpression via LMP1 pathway. This patient has optimal biomarker profile for toripalimab: EBV+, PD-L1 CPS >=50, NPC-identical histology.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Toripalimab is a humanized anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks PD-1/PD-L1 interaction, restoring anti-tumor T cell activity. EBV-driven tumors express high PD-L1 (via LMP1-mediated pathways), making them particularly susceptible to PD-1 blockade. Gemcitabine inhibits DNA synthesis via nucleoside analog incorporation. Cisplatin crosslinks DNA causing cell death. The combination of immunotherapy with chemotherapy provides synergistic anti-tumor activity through immune activation and direct cytotoxicity.

key_evidence.study_name: JUPITER-02: Toripalimab Plus Chemotherapy for R/M Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma

biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 CPS >=50 (optimal biomarker for checkpoint inhibitor response)
treatments.availability: Toripalimab FDA-approved for NPC; off-label for LEC with strong molecular rationale

treatments.name: Camrelizumab + Gemcitabine-Cisplatin (CAPTAIN-1st Protocol)

evidence_level.rationale: CAPTAIN-1st Phase 3 trial demonstrated significant PFS benefit for camrelizumab + gemcitabine-cisplatin in R/M NPC. Additionally, the DIPPER trial showed adjuvant camrelizumab improved 3-year EFS in locally advanced NPC. Applicable to LEC by NPC-analogy.

survival_benefit.rationale: Significant PFS and OS improvement in NPC. DIPPER trial showed adjuvant camrelizumab improved event-free survival in locally advanced NPC. LEC shares identical biology. PD-L1 CPS >=50 predicts enhanced benefit.

accessibility.rationale: Camrelizumab approved in China but not FDA-approved in the US. Available through clinical trials or international access programs. Toripalimab (FDA-approved) is a comparable alternative.

safety_profile.rationale: Immune-related adverse events plus chemotherapy toxicity. Camrelizumab has unique reactive capillary endothelial proliferation (RCEP) as a side effect. Manageable overall.

biomarker_match.rationale: EBV+ and PD-L1 CPS >=50 predict excellent response. Same biomarker rationale as toripalimab.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Camrelizumab is a humanized anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody. Combined with gemcitabine-cisplatin, it provides dual anti-tumor activity through immune checkpoint blockade and cytotoxic chemotherapy. EBV-driven PD-L1 overexpression makes LEC particularly susceptible to PD-1 blockade.

key_evidence.study_name: CAPTAIN-1st: Camrelizumab + GP for R/M NPC

treatments.availability: Approved in China; not FDA-approved in US. Clinical trial access.

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab (Anti-PD-1) Monotherapy or Combination

evidence_level.rationale: KEYNOTE-028 Phase 1b in R/M salivary gland cancers: ORR 12%. KEYNOTE-158 Phase 2 data available. FDA-approved for multiple indications including TMB-H tumors. PD-L1 CPS >=50 strongly predicts response in multiple tumor types. No specific LEC data but NPC-analogous biology supports use.

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 12% in salivary gland cancers overall (KEYNOTE-028), but PD-L1 CPS >=50 predicts significantly better responses across tumor types. For EBV-driven LEC with PD-L1 CPS >=50, response rate may be substantially higher. Applicable for recurrent/metastatic disease.

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for multiple indications. Widely available. PD-L1 CPS >=50 qualifies for approved indications in several tumor types. Off-label but molecularly justified for LEC.

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized immune-related adverse events. Generally manageable. Monotherapy has favorable toxicity compared to chemotherapy combinations.

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 CPS >=50 is the strongest predictive biomarker for checkpoint inhibitor response. EBV-driven PD-L1 overexpression provides biological rationale. This patient has optimal biomarker profile.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab blocks PD-1 on T cells, restoring anti-tumor immune surveillance. In EBV-driven tumors, the virus promotes PD-L1 overexpression via LMP1-mediated NF-kB and AP-1 signaling, creating an immune-suppressive microenvironment that is reversed by PD-1 blockade. PD-L1 CPS >=50 indicates high density of PD-L1 expressing cells, predicting robust immune response upon checkpoint blockade.

key_evidence.study_name: KEYNOTE-028/158: Pembrolizumab in Salivary Gland Carcinoma
key_evidence.journal: American Journal of Clinical Oncology / European Journal of Cancer

treatments.availability: FDA-approved for multiple indications; off-label for LEC

treatments.name: Definitive Chemoradiation (Without Surgery) — NPC-Like Protocol

evidence_level.rationale: Retrospective studies compare upfront surgery vs upfront chemoradiotherapy for LEC. LEC is exquisitely radiosensitive like NPC. Some series suggest definitive CRT can achieve comparable outcomes to surgery + adjuvant CRT. ESMO recommends considering definitive CRT for locally advanced, unresectable cases.

survival_benefit.rationale: Comparable outcomes to surgery + adjuvant RT in retrospective comparisons. Avoids surgical morbidity (facial nerve risk). IMRT doses of 66-70 Gy with concurrent cisplatin provide excellent local control. May be considered if surgery would require facial nerve sacrifice.

accessibility.rationale: IMRT and concurrent cisplatin widely available. NPC-like treatment protocol well-established. No surgical facility required.

safety_profile.rationale: Higher radiation dose (66-70 Gy) than adjuvant setting. Mucositis, xerostomia, dysphagia, dermatitis, cisplatin-related toxicity. Avoids surgical complications but has higher late RT toxicity.

biomarker_match.rationale: EBV-positive LEC is radiosensitive like NPC. Applicable to all LEC patients. PD-L1 status does not affect RT indication.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: High-dose IMRT (66-70 Gy) delivers curative radiation to the primary tumor and regional nodes. Concurrent cisplatin (weekly 40 mg/m2 or tri-weekly 100 mg/m2) acts as a radiosensitizer. The exquisite radiosensitivity of EBV-driven LEC (identical to NPC) means definitive CRT can achieve excellent local control without surgery. IMRT technique optimizes dose distribution while sparing contralateral salivary gland and critical structures.

key_evidence.study_name: Definitive radiotherapy as treatment option for LEC of major salivary gland: comparison of surgery vs chemoradiotherapy

biomarker_requirements: Consider for locally advanced disease where surgery would cause significant morbidity
treatments.availability: Widely available — standard NPC-like chemoradiation protocol

treatments.name: Gemcitabine + Cisplatin + Toripalimab (Neoadjuvant/Adjuvant — 2024 LEC Protocol)

evidence_level.rationale: 2024 case report of LEC treated with gemcitabine, cisplatin, and toripalimab achieving near-complete response with EBV DNA clearance. JUPITER-02 Phase 3 data supports the regimen in NPC. Novel application to salivary gland LEC.

survival_benefit.rationale: Near-complete response with EBV DNA clearance demonstrated in LEC case. JUPITER-02 showed 3-year OS of 64.5% vs 49.2% in R/M NPC. For EBV+, PD-L1 CPS >=50 LEC, this combination is molecularly rational.

accessibility.rationale: Toripalimab FDA-approved for NPC. Gemcitabine and cisplatin widely available. Combination is off-label for LEC. Limited experience in LEC specifically.

safety_profile.rationale: Case report showed no severe adverse events with 3 cycles of GP + toripalimab. Manageable toxicity profile. Immune-related AEs plus chemotherapy toxicity.

biomarker_match.rationale: EBV+ and PD-L1 CPS >=50 are ideal biomarkers. EBV DNA clearance as response marker. Molecularly optimal combination for this patient's tumor biology.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Triple combination: toripalimab (anti-PD-1 immune checkpoint blockade) plus gemcitabine (nucleoside analog causing DNA synthesis inhibition) and cisplatin (DNA crosslinking). Applied as neoadjuvant therapy before surgery/RT or as adjuvant therapy after surgery. EBV DNA monitoring serves as a real-time biomarker of treatment response. Chemotherapy-induced immunogenic cell death may enhance anti-PD-1 efficacy.

key_evidence.study_name: EBV-associated LEC treated with gemcitabine, cisplatin, toripalimab — case report (2024)

biomarker_requirements: EBV DNA monitoring for response assessment
treatments.availability: Available at academic oncology centers; off-label for LEC

treatments.name: Ipilimumab + Nivolumab (Dual Checkpoint Blockade) — For Recurrent/Metastatic Disease

evidence_level.rationale: Ongoing clinical trial of ipilimumab + nivolumab in recurrent/metastatic salivary gland cancers with results expected May 2025. No specific LEC data. Dual checkpoint blockade (anti-CTLA-4 + anti-PD-1) has shown efficacy in multiple tumor types.

survival_benefit.rationale: Dual checkpoint blockade achieves higher response rates than single-agent anti-PD-1 in multiple cancers. For PD-L1 CPS >=50 EBV-driven LEC, this combination may provide enhanced immune activation. Results pending.

accessibility.rationale: Both agents FDA-approved for multiple indications. Available through clinical trials or off-label use. Higher cost than single-agent immunotherapy.

safety_profile.rationale: Dual checkpoint blockade has significantly higher immune-related adverse event rates than single-agent therapy. Grade 3-4 AEs in 30-60% of patients across trials. Requires experienced immune oncology management.

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 CPS >=50 predicts excellent response. Dual blockade may overcome resistance mechanisms. EBV-driven tumors have immune-inflamed microenvironment amenable to immunotherapy.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Ipilimumab blocks CTLA-4 on T cells, enhancing T cell priming and activation in lymph nodes. Nivolumab blocks PD-1, restoring anti-tumor T cell effector function in the tumor microenvironment. The combination provides complementary immune checkpoint blockade at different stages of the anti-tumor immune response, leading to synergistic anti-tumor activity.

key_evidence.study_name: Ipilimumab + Nivolumab in R/M Salivary Gland Carcinoma (ongoing trial)

notable_side_effects: High rate of immune-related AEs (30-60% grade 3-4)
treatments.availability: Both agents FDA-approved; combination available through clinical trials or off-label

treatments.name: EBV-Specific Cytotoxic T Lymphocyte (CTL) Adoptive Cell Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: VANCE Phase 3 trial (largest adoptive T cell trial in solid tumors, 330 patients) evaluated EBV-CTL + chemotherapy in R/M NPC. Addition of EBV-CTL did not improve OS over chemotherapy alone (25.0 vs 24.9 months). However, excellent safety profile. Earlier phase trials showed anti-tumor activity.

survival_benefit.rationale: VANCE Phase 3 trial showed no OS improvement (25.0 vs 24.9 months). Despite theoretical rationale, EBV-specific CTL did not add benefit beyond standard chemotherapy in R/M NPC. May have role in different clinical settings (maintenance, minimal residual disease).

accessibility.rationale: Requires specialized cell therapy manufacturing. Limited to academic research centers with GMP cell production facilities. Not FDA-approved. Experimental only.

safety_profile.rationale: Excellent safety. Only 1 subject experienced a grade 2 serious adverse event related to EBV-CTL in VANCE trial. International trans-shipment from collection to manufacturing achieved for >90% of patients.

biomarker_match.rationale: EBV-positive tumor expressing viral antigens (LMP1, LMP2, EBNA1) is the ideal target for EBV-specific CTL. Directly targets the viral driver of the tumor. Applicable to all EBV+ malignancies.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Autologous or allogeneic T lymphocytes are expanded ex vivo against EBV viral antigens (LMP1, LMP2, EBNA1) expressed on tumor cells. Infused EBV-specific CTLs recognize and kill EBV-positive tumor cells via MHC-restricted cytotoxicity. The approach directly targets the viral etiology of the cancer.

key_evidence.study_name: VANCE: Phase 3 trial of GC + EBV-CTL vs GC alone in R/M NPC

biomarker_requirements: EBV viral antigen expression (LMP1, LMP2, EBNA1)
notable_side_effects: Very low toxicity — Grade 2 SAE in 1/330 patients
treatments.availability: Experimental — research centers with GMP cell therapy facilities only

treatments.name: Induction Gemcitabine-Cisplatin Followed by Concurrent Chemoradiation

evidence_level.rationale: NEJM-published Phase 3 trial demonstrated induction GP + CRT improved recurrence-free survival vs CRT alone in locoregionally advanced NPC. GEM20110714 confirmed GP as first-line standard. Applicable to LEC by NPC-analogy.

survival_benefit.rationale: Induction GP reduced distant metastasis risk in NPC. GP is superior to FP as first-line for R/M NPC (Phase 3). For T3N1 LEC, induction chemotherapy can address micrometastatic disease before definitive local therapy.

accessibility.rationale: Gemcitabine and cisplatin widely available. Standard NPC induction protocol. IMRT for concurrent phase widely available.

safety_profile.rationale: Induction chemotherapy toxicity (myelosuppression, nephrotoxicity, nausea) followed by concurrent chemoradiation toxicity (mucositis, xerostomia). Sequential approach results in cumulative toxicity over 4-5 months of treatment.

biomarker_match.rationale: EBV-positive LEC shares NPC biology. Standard approach for all NPC/LEC regardless of PD-L1 status.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Induction phase: gemcitabine (1 g/m2 days 1 and 8) + cisplatin (80 mg/m2 day 1) every 3 weeks for 3 cycles to achieve tumor shrinkage and address micrometastases. Concurrent phase: weekly cisplatin (40 mg/m2) with IMRT (66-70 Gy). The induction phase reduces tumor burden before definitive local therapy, potentially improving local control and reducing distant failure.

key_evidence.study_name: Gemcitabine and Cisplatin Induction Chemotherapy in NPC (NEJM)

treatments.availability: Widely available — standard NPC induction protocol

treatments.name: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab — For Recurrent/Metastatic Disease

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data in R/M salivary gland cancers (ASCO 2025). No specific LEC data. Lenvatinib modulates immune microenvironment to enhance checkpoint inhibitor efficacy.

survival_benefit.rationale: Emerging data from ASCO 2025. For PD-L1 CPS >=50 LEC, pembrolizumab component may provide significant benefit. Lenvatinib adds anti-angiogenic effects.

accessibility.rationale: Both agents individually FDA-approved. Off-label combination for LEC.

safety_profile.rationale: Additive toxicity from lenvatinib (hypertension, diarrhea) and pembrolizumab (immune-related AEs).

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 CPS >=50 predicts pembrolizumab response. Lenvatinib may enhance immune response through microenvironment modulation.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Lenvatinib (multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR/FGFR) modulates tumor immune microenvironment by reducing immunosuppressive cells and increasing T cell infiltration. Pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1) restores anti-tumor T cell activity. Synergistic immune activation through complementary mechanisms.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II study of lenvatinib + pembrolizumab in R/M salivary gland cancers

treatments.availability: Both agents FDA-approved individually; combination off-label

treatments.name: Camrelizumab + Apatinib (Anti-PD-1 + Anti-VEGFR2) — For Recurrent/Metastatic

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data in NPC with platinum-resistant R/M disease. Notable disease control. Phase 2 data in N3-stage NPC with chemotherapy (Nature Communications 2024). Applicable to LEC by NPC-analogy.

survival_benefit.rationale: Disease control in platinum-resistant NPC. Survival benefit in N3-stage NPC combined with chemotherapy. For PD-L1 CPS >=50 EBV+ LEC, may provide salvage option after progression on initial therapy.

accessibility.rationale: Approved in China. Not FDA-approved in US. Available through clinical trials or international access programs.

safety_profile.rationale: Camrelizumab RCEP (reactive capillary endothelial proliferation), apatinib-related hypertension, hand-foot syndrome, proteinuria. Immune-related AEs.

biomarker_match.rationale: EBV+ and PD-L1 CPS >=50 predict checkpoint inhibitor response. Apatinib targets VEGFR2 which is involved in tumor angiogenesis.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Camrelizumab (anti-PD-1) restores anti-tumor immunity. Apatinib selectively inhibits VEGFR2, reducing tumor angiogenesis and modulating the tumor immune microenvironment. The combination provides synergistic anti-tumor activity through immune checkpoint blockade and anti-angiogenic effects.

key_evidence.study_name: Camrelizumab + apatinib + induction chemo + CRT in N3-stage NPC (Phase 2)

treatments.availability: Approved in China; clinical trial access internationally

clinical_trials.title: Toripalimab Plus Gemcitabine-Cisplatin for Recurrent/Metastatic Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma
biomarker_requirements: R/M nasopharyngeal carcinoma (applicable to LEC by NPC-analogy)
clinical_trials.early_results: PFS 21.4 vs 8.2 months (HR 0.52). 3-year OS 64.5% vs 49.2%. FDA approval for toripalimab in NPC based on these results.

clinical_trials.title: Camrelizumab + Gemcitabine-Cisplatin for R/M NPC
clinical_trials.early_results: Significant PFS benefit for camrelizumab + GP vs placebo + GP.

clinical_trials.title: Dual Checkpoint Blockade (Ipilimumab + Nivolumab) in Recurrent/Metastatic Salivary Gland Cancers
biomarker_requirements: R/M salivary gland cancer (all histologies including LEC)
clinical_trials.early_results: Results pending. Dual checkpoint blockade in SGC.

clinical_trials.title: Gemcitabine/Carboplatin + EBV-Specific CTL vs GC Alone in R/M NPC
clinical_trials.early_results: Negative: EBV-CTL did not improve OS (25.0 vs 24.9 months). Excellent safety profile.

clinical_trials.title: Adjuvant Camrelizumab Following CRT in Locally Advanced NPC
clinical_trials.early_results: Adjuvant camrelizumab significantly improved 3-year event-free survival in locally advanced NPC.

clinical_trials.title: Short-Course PD-1 Blockade (Toripalimab) in Locally Advanced NPC
clinical_trials.early_results: Protocol published. Testing short-course neoadjuvant toripalimab before definitive CRT.

combination_strategies.evidence_level: Standard of Care — ASCO/ESMO guidelines, multiple retrospective series
combination_strategies.rationale: Established multimodality approach for T3N1 LEC. Surgery removes gross disease; adjuvant CRT eliminates microscopic residual disease. LEC is exquisitely radiosensitive (NPC-analogous). 5-year OS 90-97% with this approach.

NPC-analogous treatment for EBV-driven LEC. JUPITER-02 demonstrated PFS tripling (21.4 vs 8.2 months) and OS improvement (64.5% vs 49.2% 3-year). PD-L1 CPS >=50 predicts enhanced benefit from checkpoint inhibitor component. EBV drives PD-L1 overexpression, creating optimal biology for combined immunochemotherapy.

Sequential approach for locally advanced EBV-driven cancer. Induction chemotherapy reduces tumor burden and addresses micrometastases before definitive CRT. NEJM Phase 3 in NPC demonstrated recurrence-free survival benefit (HR 0.51). Applicable to T3N1 LEC with identical biology.

combination_strategies.evidence_level: Phase 3 (DIPPER trial) — adjuvant camrelizumab in locally advanced NPC
combination_strategies.rationale: After definitive treatment with surgery + CRT, adjuvant anti-PD-1 maintenance may prevent recurrence. DIPPER trial showed adjuvant camrelizumab improved 3-year EFS in locally advanced NPC. PD-L1 CPS >=50 predicts sustained benefit. EBV DNA monitoring can guide therapy duration.

For recurrent/metastatic LEC. Lenvatinib enhances anti-tumor immunity by modulating tumor microenvironment. PD-L1 CPS >=50 predicts pembrolizumab response. Phase 2 data in R/M SGC presented at ASCO 2025.

supportive_care.approach: EBV DNA Monitoring (Plasma EBV DNA as Biomarker)
supportive_care.evidence: EBV DNA is a validated circulating biomarker for NPC and EBV-driven cancers. Pre-treatment levels correlate with disease burden. Post-treatment clearance indicates response. Rising levels may herald recurrence before radiographic detection. 2024 LEC case demonstrated EBV DNA clearance with successful treatment.
supportive_care.benefit: Real-time monitoring of treatment response. Early detection of recurrence or progression. Non-invasive liquid biopsy approach. Guides treatment decisions including duration of immunotherapy maintenance.

supportive_care.approach: Nutritional Support and Dietitian Consultation During CRT
supportive_care.evidence: ESPEN guidelines for head and neck cancer patients receiving chemoradiation. Prophylactic PEG tube placement recommended for patients receiving concurrent chemoradiation to the head and neck. Weight loss during CRT correlates with treatment interruptions and worse outcomes.
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains nutritional status during 7-week CRT course. Reduces treatment interruptions. Improves treatment tolerance. PEG tube provides reliable enteral access if severe mucositis develops.

supportive_care.evidence: MASCC/ISOO guidelines for prevention and management of oral mucositis during head and neck CRT. Cryotherapy, benzydamine rinse, and palifermin reduce mucositis severity.
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces grade 3-4 mucositis incidence. Maintains oral intake. Reduces treatment interruptions. Improves quality of life during CRT.

supportive_care.evidence: ASCO/NCCN guidelines for management of immune-related adverse events. Essential when checkpoint inhibitors (toripalimab, pembrolizumab) are incorporated. Baseline thyroid function, LFTs, and inflammatory markers should be monitored.
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection and management of irAEs preserves checkpoint inhibitor treatment duration. Prevents life-threatening complications. Standardized grading and management algorithms improve outcomes.

supportive_care.approach: Facial Nerve Rehabilitation (If Nerve Compromised by Surgery)
supportive_care.evidence: Standard of care after parotidectomy with facial nerve involvement. Options include nerve grafting, gold weight implant for eye closure, and physical therapy.
supportive_care.benefit: Restores facial function, eye protection, and social functioning. Important for quality of life in 45-year-old patient.

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Distress Management guidelines. EBV-associated LEC has ethnic predisposition (Southeast Asian, Inuit, North African). Cultural sensitivity in communication about prognosis and treatment options.
supportive_care.benefit: Addresses cancer-related distress. Culturally appropriate support. Communicates favorable prognosis (5-year OS >90%). Supports treatment adherence.
